LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO ASSESSOR-RECORDER, SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION: THIS TRANSFER TAX REFUND CASE IS BEFORE THE COURT ON CROSS MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE DEMAND LETTERS FOR TRANSFER TAX PAYMENT RELY ON "THE CITY'S BUSINESS AND TAX REGULATIONS CODE ARTICLE 12-C, SEC. 1108." THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT NOTES THIS RELIANCE AND ASSERTS: "NO OTHER BASIS FOR TAX HAS BEEN ALLEGED" BY THE CITY. (FAC 71.) AS THE CITY'S SUMMARY ADJUDICATION PAPERS NOW RELY ON MULTIPLE OTHER LOCAL AND STATE LAWS AS BASES FOR TAX, THIS RAISES THE GATING ISSUE OF WHETHER THE CITY MAY SO RELY - AN ISSUE NEITHER SIDE'S PAPERS APPEAR TO ADDRESS. THUS, BOTH SIDES ARE TO ADDRESS THIS DISCRETE ISSUE IN BRIEFS TO BE FILED BY JULY 31, 2020. THE HEARING ON THE PENDING MOTIONS IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 20, 2020 AT 1:30 P.M. IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Continued To Aug-20-20 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs CIM URBAN REIT 211 MAIN STREET (SF), LP A, CIM URBAN REIT PROPERTIES II, LP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication:

This transfer tax refund case is before the court on cross motions for summary adjudication. The demand letters for transfer tax payment rely on "the City's Business and Tax Regulations Code Article 12-C, Sec. 1108." The first amended complaint notes this reliance and asserts: "No other basis for tax has been alleged" by the City. (FAC 71.) As the City's summary adjudication papers now rely on multiple other local and state laws as bases for tax, this raises the gating issue of whether the City may so rely - an issue neither side's papers appear to address. Thus, both sides are to address this discrete issue in briefs to be filed by July 31, 2020. The hearing on the pending motions is continued to August 20, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 302.
